other_observers: Dan Maynard, Peter Gaede, Mark Peterson, Glenn Walbek, Loch Kilpatrick; Dan found the first bird and the rest of us circled it for better viewing.
description: small sparrow with a yellow face when flushed and short spiky tail. Perched, it had a broad yellow/orange eye stripe, a contrasty gray auricular with orangish/yellow wash below. Face with a thin dark line runing behind the eye ending in a dark triangle. Crown with a thin white central crown stipe surrounded by two broader blackish ones. Nape light grayish with some very thin parallel browns stipes in it. Back richly patterned with alternating wide rows of browns and whites, also noticeable in flight. Breast with an orange wash with thin brownish steaks continuing to flanks. Over all beautiful bird.Behaviors: Very skulky with very short low flight just over the grassland habitat. On the ground was curiouis but always kept some vegetation between it and the observers.Call: call distinctive from other sparrows in the area but I don't remember it well enough to describePlumage: adult basic
